text: This is the VOA Special English Technology Report.
zh: 这是美国之音特别英语科技报道。

text: A team of scientists in the United States has created a new kind of mouse that has an immune system similar to that of humans.
zh: 美国一个科学家团队培育出一种新型小鼠,它拥有与人类相似的免疫系统。

text: The scientists hope their research with these mice will speed up development of a vaccine to prevent human
zh: 科学家希望,他们对这些小鼠的研究能加快研发一种预防人类,

text: immunodeficiency virus, or HIV.
zh: 免疫缺陷病毒(即HIV)的疫苗。

text: Scientists from the Reagan Institute of Massachusetts General Hospital,
zh: 来自麻省总医院拉根研究所、

text: Massachusetts Institute of Technology, and Harvard are carrying out the new research.
zh: 麻省理工学院和哈佛大学的科学家正在开展这项新研究。

text: Earlier research has shown that certain individuals with HIV have immune systems that do better at controlling the AIDS virus.
zh: 早先的研究表明,某些感染HIV的人,其免疫系统在控制艾滋病病毒方面表现更好。

text: These individuals are commonly known as elite controllers.
zh: 这些人通常被称为『精英控制者』。

text: They often live longer with the virus and have fewer problems early on.
zh: 他们带毒往往能活得更久,早期出现的问题也更少。

text: Todd Todd Allen is one of the lead authors of the new study, which was published in Science Translational Medicine.
zh: 托德·艾伦是这项新研究的主要作者之一,该研究发表在《科学·转化医学》上。

text: He says some people are able to control HIV very well.
zh: 他说,有些人能够非常好地控制HIV。

text: By using the new experimental mice,
zh: 通过使用这种新的实验小鼠,

text: the researchers hope to learn what it is about the immune systems of these elite controllers that causes them
zh: 研究人员希望弄清,这些『精英控制者』的免疫系统究竟有何特别之处,使他们,

text: to deal with the HIV virus better than others.
zh: 比别人更好地对付HIV病毒。

text: The humanized mice were created using stem cells and tissue from human donors.
zh: 这些『人源化』小鼠是用人类捐赠者的干细胞和组织培育出来的。

text: Some of the tissue was taken from the liver and thymus.
zh: 部分组织取自肝脏和胸腺。

text: The thymus is a large gland at the bottom of the throat.
zh: 胸腺是位于咽喉底部的一个大腺体。

text: It trains T lymphocytes, or T cells, to attack unwelcome microbes.
zh: 它训练T淋巴细胞(即T细胞)去攻击不受欢迎的微生物。

text: In that way, it protects the body from infection.
zh: 以这种方式,它保护身体免受感染。

text: When the scientists infected the so -called humanized mice with the HIV virus,
zh: 当科学家用HIV病毒感染这些所谓的『人源化』小鼠时,

text: the T cell reaction in the mice was exactly the same as that of humans.
zh: 小鼠体内的T细胞反应与人类的完全相同。

text: Earlier research using rhesus monkeys helped scientists understand how the virus attacks cells.
zh: 早先利用恒河猴的研究,帮助科学家理解病毒是如何攻击细胞的。

text: These monkeys were seen as good replacements for humans because they could easily be infected with a primate version of HIV HIV,
zh: 这些猴子曾被视为人类的良好替代者,因为它们很容易感染一种灵长类版本的HIV,

text: known as SIV.
zh: 即SIV。

text: But genetic differences in the two versions of the virus
zh: 但这两种病毒版本,

text: and the immune systems suggested that monkeys were not the best candidates for HIV research.
zh: 以及免疫系统之间的基因差异表明,猴子并非HIV研究的最佳对象。

text: Todd Allen Todd Allen says the experiments with the new humanized mouse did a better job of showing what happens in the human body
zh: 托德·艾伦说,用这种新的『人源化』小鼠做实验,能更好地展现人体内,

text: with the AIDS virus.
zh: 面对艾滋病病毒时发生的情况。

text: He says the experimental mouse permits researchers
zh: 他说,这种实验小鼠让研究人员,

text: to test the discoveries they have made about individuals infected with HIV.
zh: 得以验证他们对HIV感染者所获得的发现。

text: Todd Allen and the other researchers hope further studies with the humanized mouse will lead to an HIV vaccine.
zh: 托德·艾伦和其他研究人员希望,对『人源化』小鼠的进一步研究能带来一种HIV疫苗。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Carolyn Persutti.
zh: 美国之音特别英语,我是卡罗琳·普雷苏蒂。
